steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage says that few of those farmers had actually seen Haudenosaunee farms firsthand, and it explicitly rules out independent reinvention. This sets up a transmission puzzle: if the farmers neither observed the techniques directly nor invented them on their own, the techniques must have reached them through some other route.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice A explains the spread via intermediaries — people "more directly influenced" by Haudenosaunee practices who passed the techniques along. This is the only route the passage leaves open once direct observation and independent invention are excluded.
  • Choice A — "learned from other people": fills the transmission gap left by the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — "crops not well suited": shifts focus to crops, not the spread of techniques. ✗
  • Choice C — "widely used in regions outside": addresses geography elsewhere, not how northeastern farmers obtained the techniques. ✗
  • Choice D — "late in the nineteenth century": contradicts the passage's "early nineteenth century" timeframe. ✗
